title: Review and Next Steps
subtitle: Recap everything you have learned and plan your Python journey
learning_objectives: Apply multiple Python concepts in one capstone program
Know what to learn next after this course
Feel confident continuing your Python journey

sections.heading: Everything You Have Learned
sections.content:
You have covered an enormous amount of ground. Here is a summary of what you now know:

Module 1-2: Variables, strings, numbers, booleans, f-strings, type conversion
Module 3: if/elif/else, while loops, for loops, range(), break, continue
Module 4: Lists, tuples, dictionaries, sets, nested collections
Module 5: Functions, parameters, return values, scope, lambda, recursion
Module 6: String methods, comprehensions, file I/O, JSON
Module 7: try/except, error types, raise, custom exceptions
Module 8: Classes, objects, __init__, methods, inheritance, dunder methods
Module 9: math, random, datetime, os modules, pip, PyPI
Module 10: Real programs: games, calculators, text analysis, algorithms

sections.heading: What to Learn Next
sections.content:
You have a solid Python foundation. Here are excellent next steps depending on what interests you:

Web Development: Learn Flask or Django to build websites and APIs
Data Science: Learn pandas, numpy, and matplotlib for data analysis
Automation: Use os, shutil, pathlib to automate repetitive tasks
Machine Learning: Learn scikit-learn and TensorFlow / PyTorch
Games: Use pygame to build 2D games

The best way to keep learning is to build projects you care about. Every project teaches you something new.
sections.code_example:
# Recommended resources:
# - docs.python.org (official Python documentation)
# - realpython.com (excellent tutorials)
# - exercism.org/tracks/python (coding exercises)
# - projecteuler.net (math + programming challenges)
# - kaggle.com (data science projects and competitions)

print("You are now a Python programmer!")
print("Keep building, keep learning.")
sections.code_output:
You are now a Python programmer!
Keep building, keep learning.

exercises.title: Capstone: Grade Tracker
exercises.prompt: Write a Student class with __init__(name), add_grade(grade) that appends to self.grades, average() that returns the mean, and __str__ returning 'Name: avg X'. Create a student, add grades [85, 92, 78, 96], and print them.
hints: The starter code is complete — just run it!
It combines OOP, lists, functions, f-strings, and maths

exercises.title: Your Python Journey
exercises.prompt: Write code that prints a personalised summary: your name, how many modules you completed (10), and one thing you want to build with Python.
exercises.starter_code:
# Create a variable called my_name and assign your name to it
# Create a variable called modules_completed and set it to 10
# Create a variable called what_i_want_to_build and assign what you want to build

# Print three lines using f-strings:
# "My name is {my_name}."
# "I completed {modules_completed} Python modules."
# "Next, I want to build {what_i_want_to_build}!"
exercises.solution:
my_name = "Your Name"
modules_completed = 10
what_i_want_to_build = "a web app"

print(f"My name is {my_name}.")
print(f"I completed {modules_completed} Python modules.")
print(f"Next, I want to build {what_i_want_to_build}!")
hints: Change my_name to your actual name
Then run the code and celebrate!

quiz.question: What is the best way to continue improving at Python after this course?
options: Read more textbooks without writing any code
Build projects you are personally interested in and look things up as you go
Wait until you know everything before starting a project
quiz.explanation: The best way to learn programming is to build things. Projects give you real problems to solve, which forces you to look things up and learn. You don't need to know everything first — programmers look things up constantly.

quiz.question: Which of the following is a good next step if you are interested in building websites?
quiz.explanation: Flask is a lightweight framework for building web applications with Python. Django is more feature-complete and powers many large websites. Both are excellent starting points for web development.

completion_message: CONGRATULATIONS! You have completed the entire Learn Python course! You are now a Python programmer. Go build something amazing!
